00020 
00021 package jelatine;
00022 
00023 import java.util.Calendar;
00024 import java.util.TimeZone;
00025 
00029 public final class VMTimeZone extends TimeZone
00030 {
00035     private int rawOffset;
00036 
00040     private boolean useDaylight;
00041 
00047     private int dstSavings = 60 * 60 * 1000;
00048 
00052     private int startYear;
00053     private static final int DOM_MODE = 1;
00054     private static final int DOW_IN_MONTH_MODE = 2;
00055     private static final int DOW_GE_DOM_MODE = 3;
00056     private static final int DOW_LE_DOM_MODE = 4;
00057 
00085     private int startMode;
00086 
00091     private int startMonth;
00092 
00096     private int startDay;
00097 
00102     private int startDayOfWeek;
00103 
00109     private int startTime;
00110 
00116     private int startTimeMode = WALL_TIME;
00117 
00122     private int endMonth;
00123 
00128     private int endMode;
00129 
00133     private int endDay;
00134 
00139     private int endDayOfWeek;
00140 
00146     private int endTime;
00147 
00152     private int endTimeMode = WALL_TIME;
00153 
00162 //    private byte[] monthLength = monthArr;
00163     private static final byte[] monthArr = { 31, 28, 31, 30, 31, 30, 31, 31, 30,
00164         31, 30, 31 };
00165 
00170     public static final int STANDARD_TIME = 1;
00171 
00176     public static final int WALL_TIME = 0;
00177 
00181     public static final int UTC_TIME = 2;
00182 
00189     public VMTimeZone(int rawOffset, String id)
00190     {
00191         this.rawOffset = rawOffset;
00192         useDaylight = false;
00193         startYear = 0;
00194     }
00195 
00202     public void setStartYear(int year)
00203     {
00204         startYear = year;
00205         useDaylight = true;
00206     }
00207 
00217     private int checkRule(int month, int day, int dayOfWeek)
00218     {
00219         if (month < 0 || month > 11)
00220             throw new IllegalArgumentException();
00221 
00222         int daysInMonth = getDaysInMonth(month, 1);
00223 
00224         if (dayOfWeek == 0)
00225         {
00226             if (day <= 0 || day > daysInMonth)
00227                 throw new IllegalArgumentException();
00228 
00229             return DOM_MODE;
00230         }
00231         else if (dayOfWeek > 0)
00232         {
00233             if (Math.abs(day) > (daysInMonth + 6) / 7)
00234                 throw new IllegalArgumentException();
00235 
00236             if (dayOfWeek > Calendar.SATURDAY)
00237                 throw new IllegalArgumentException();
00238 
00239             return DOW_IN_MONTH_MODE;
00240         }
00241         else
00242         {
00243             if (day == 0 || Math.abs(day) > daysInMonth)
00244                 throw new IllegalArgumentException();
00245             if (dayOfWeek < -Calendar.SATURDAY)
00246                 throw new IllegalArgumentException();
00247             if (day < 0)
00248                 return DOW_LE_DOM_MODE;
00249             else
00250                 return DOW_GE_DOM_MODE;
00251         }
00252     }
00253 
00268     public void setStartRule(int month, int day, int dayOfWeek, int time)
00269     {
00270         this.startMode = checkRule(month, day, dayOfWeek);
00271         this.startMonth = month;
00272         this.startDay = day;
00273         this.startDayOfWeek = Math.abs(dayOfWeek);
00274 
00275         if (this.startTimeMode == WALL_TIME
00276             || this.startTimeMode == STANDARD_TIME)
00277         {
00278             this.startTime = time;
00279         }
00280         else
00281         {
00282             // Convert from UTC to STANDARD
00283             this.startTime = time + this.rawOffset;
00284         }
00285 
00286         useDaylight = true;
00287     }
00288 
00312     public void setStartRule(int month, int day, int dayOfWeek, int time,
00313         boolean after)
00314     {
00315         // FIXME: XXX: Validate that checkRule and offset processing work with
00316         // on or before mode.
00317         this.startDay = after ? Math.abs(day) : -Math.abs(day);
00318         this.startDayOfWeek =
00319             after ? Math.abs(dayOfWeek) : -Math.abs(dayOfWeek);
00320         this.startMode = (dayOfWeek != 0)
00321              ? (after ? DOW_GE_DOM_MODE : DOW_LE_DOM_MODE)
00322               : checkRule(month, day, dayOfWeek);
00323         this.startDay = Math.abs(this.startDay);
00324         this.startDayOfWeek = Math.abs(this.startDayOfWeek);
00325 
00326         this.startMonth = month;
00327 
00328         if (this.startTimeMode == WALL_TIME
00329             || this.startTimeMode == STANDARD_TIME)
00330         {
00331             this.startTime = time;
00332         }
00333         else
00334         {
00335             // Convert from UTC to STANDARD
00336             this.startTime = time + this.rawOffset;
00337         }
00338 
00339         useDaylight = true;
00340     }
00341 
00354     public void setStartRule(int month, int day, int time)
00355     {
00356         setStartRule(month, day, 0, time);
00357     }
00358 
00370     public void setEndRule(int month, int day, int dayOfWeek, int time)
00371     {
00372         this.endMode = checkRule(month, day, dayOfWeek);
00373         this.endMonth = month;
00374         this.endDay = day;
00375         this.endDayOfWeek = Math.abs(dayOfWeek);
00376 
00377         if (this.endTimeMode == WALL_TIME)
00378             this.endTime = time;
00379         else if (this.endTimeMode == STANDARD_TIME)
00380         {
00381             // Convert from STANDARD to DST
00382             this.endTime = time + this.dstSavings;
00383         }
00384         else
00385         {
00386             // Convert from UTC to DST
00387             this.endTime = time + this.rawOffset + this.dstSavings;
00388         }
00389 
00390         useDaylight = true;
00391     }
00392 
00414     public void setEndRule(int month, int day, int dayOfWeek, int time,
00415         boolean after)
00416     {
00417         // FIXME: XXX: Validate that checkRule and offset processing work with
00418         // on or before mode.
00419         this.endDay = after ? Math.abs(day) : -Math.abs(day);
00420         this.endDayOfWeek = after ? Math.abs(dayOfWeek) : -Math.abs(dayOfWeek);
00421         this.endMode = (dayOfWeek != 0)
00422              ? (after ? DOW_GE_DOM_MODE : DOW_LE_DOM_MODE)
00423              : checkRule(month, day, dayOfWeek);
00424         this.endDay = Math.abs(this.endDay);
00425         this.endDayOfWeek = Math.abs(endDayOfWeek);
00426 
00427         this.endMonth = month;
00428 
00429         if (this.endTimeMode == WALL_TIME)
00430             this.endTime = time;
00431         else if (this.endTimeMode == STANDARD_TIME)
00432         {
00433             // Convert from STANDARD to DST
00434             this.endTime = time + this.dstSavings;
00435         }
00436         else
00437         {
00438             // Convert from UTC to DST
00439             this.endTime = time + this.rawOffset + this.dstSavings;
00440         }
00441 
00442         useDaylight = true;
00443     }
00444 
00455     public void setEndRule(int month, int day, int time)
00456     {
00457         setEndRule(month, day, 0, time);
00458     }
00459 
00487     public int getOffset(int era, int year, int month, int day, int dayOfWeek,
00488         int millis)
00489     {
00490         int daysInMonth = getDaysInMonth(month, year);
00491 
00492         if (day < 1 || day > daysInMonth)
00493             throw new IllegalArgumentException();
00494 
00495         if (dayOfWeek < Calendar.SUNDAY || dayOfWeek > Calendar.SATURDAY)
00496             throw new IllegalArgumentException();
00497 
00498         if (month < Calendar.JANUARY || month > Calendar.DECEMBER)
00499             throw new IllegalArgumentException();
00500 
00501         // This method is called by Calendar, so we mustn't use that class.
00502         int daylightSavings = 0;
00503 
00504         if (useDaylight && era == 1 && year >= startYear)
00505         {
00506             // This does only work for Gregorian calendars :-(
00507             // This is mainly because setStartYear doesn't take an era.
00508             boolean afterStart = ! isBefore(year, month, day, dayOfWeek, millis,
00509                 startMode, startMonth, startDay, startDayOfWeek, startTime);
00510             boolean beforeEnd = isBefore(year, month, day, dayOfWeek,
00511                 millis + dstSavings, endMode, endMonth, endDay, endDayOfWeek,
00512                 endTime);
00513 
00514             if (startMonth < endMonth)
00515             {
00516                 // use daylight savings, if the date is after the start of
00517                 // savings, and before the end of savings.
00518                 daylightSavings = afterStart && beforeEnd ? dstSavings : 0;
00519             }
00520             else
00521             {
00522                 // use daylight savings, if the date is before the end of
00523                 // savings, or after the start of savings.
00524                 daylightSavings = beforeEnd || afterStart ? dstSavings : 0;
00525             }
00526         }
00527 
00528         return rawOffset + daylightSavings;
00529     }
00530 
00536     public int getRawOffset()
00537     {
00538         return rawOffset;
00539     }
00540 
00545     public void setRawOffset(int rawOffset)
00546     {
00547         this.rawOffset = rawOffset;
00548     }
00549 
00556     public int getDSTSavings()
00557     {
00558         return dstSavings;
00559     }
00560 
00567     public void setDSTSavings(int dstSavings)
00568     {
00569         if (dstSavings <= 0)
00570             throw new IllegalArgumentException();
00571 
00572         this.dstSavings = dstSavings;
00573     }
00574 
00579     public boolean useDaylightTime()
00580     {
00581         return useDaylight;
00582     }
00583 
00590     private int getDaysInMonth(int month, int year)
00591     {
00592         if (month == Calendar.FEBRUARY)
00593         {
00594             if ((year & 3) != 0)
00595                 return 28;
00596 
00597             // Assume default Gregorian cutover,
00598             // all years prior to this must be Julian
00599             if (year < 1582)
00600                 return 29;
00601 
00602             // Gregorian rules
00603             return ((year % 100) != 0 || (year % 400) == 0) ? 29 : 28;
00604         }
00605         else
00606             return monthArr[month];
00607     }
00608 
00626     private boolean isBefore(int calYear, int calMonth, int calDayOfMonth,
00627         int calDayOfWeek, int calMillis, int mode, int month, int day,
00628         int dayOfWeek, int millis)
00629     {
00630         // This method is called by Calendar, so we mustn't use that class.
00631         // We have to do all calculations by hand.
00632         // check the months:
00633         // XXX - this is not correct:
00634         // for the DOW_GE_DOM and DOW_LE_DOM modes the change date may
00635         // be in a different month.
00636         if (calMonth != month)
00637             return calMonth < month;
00638 
00639         // check the day:
00640         switch (mode)
00641         {
00642             case DOM_MODE:
00643                 if (calDayOfMonth != day)
00644                     return calDayOfMonth < day;
00645 
00646                 break;
00647             case DOW_IN_MONTH_MODE:
00648             {
00649                 // This computes the day of month of the day of type
00650                 // "dayOfWeek" that lies in the same (sunday based) week as cal.
00651                 calDayOfMonth += (dayOfWeek - calDayOfWeek);
00652 
00653                 // Now we convert it to 7 based number (to get a one based offset
00654                 // after dividing by 7).  If we count from the end of the
00655                 // month, we get want a -7 based number counting the days from
00656                 // the end:
00657                 if (day < 0)
00658                     calDayOfMonth -= getDaysInMonth(calMonth, calYear) + 7;
00659                 else
00660                     calDayOfMonth += 6;
00661 
00662                 //  day > 0                    day < 0
00663                 //  S  M  T  W  T  F  S        S  M  T  W  T  F  S
00664                 //     7  8  9 10 11 12         -36-35-34-33-32-31
00665                 // 13 14 15 16 17 18 19      -30-29-28-27-26-25-24
00666                 // 20 21 22 23 24 25 26      -23-22-21-20-19-18-17
00667                 // 27 28 29 30 31 32 33      -16-15-14-13-12-11-10
00668                 // 34 35 36                   -9 -8 -7
00669                 // Now we calculate the day of week in month:
00670                 int week = calDayOfMonth / 7;
00671 
00672                 //  day > 0                    day < 0
00673                 //  S  M  T  W  T  F  S        S  M  T  W  T  F  S
00674                 //     1  1  1  1  1  1          -5 -5 -4 -4 -4 -4
00675                 //  1  2  2  2  2  2  2       -4 -4 -4 -3 -3 -3 -3
00676                 //  2  3  3  3  3  3  3       -3 -3 -3 -2 -2 -2 -2
00677                 //  3  4  4  4  4  4  4       -2 -2 -2 -1 -1 -1 -1
00678                 //  4  5  5                   -1 -1 -1
00679                 if (week != day)
00680                     return week < day;
00681 
00682                 if (calDayOfWeek != dayOfWeek)
00683                     return calDayOfWeek < dayOfWeek;
00684 
00685                 // daylight savings starts/ends  on the given day.
00686                 break;
00687             }
00688             case DOW_LE_DOM_MODE:
00689                 // The greatest sunday before or equal December, 12
00690                 // is the same as smallest sunday after or equal December, 6.
00691                 day = Math.abs(day) - 6;
00692             case DOW_GE_DOM_MODE:
00693                 // Calculate the day of month of the day of type
00694                 // "dayOfWeek" that lies before (or on) the given date.
00695                 calDayOfMonth -= (calDayOfWeek < dayOfWeek ? 7 : 0)
00696                     + calDayOfWeek - dayOfWeek;
00697                 if (calDayOfMonth < day)
00698                     return true;
00699 
00700                 if (calDayOfWeek != dayOfWeek || calDayOfMonth >= day + 7)
00701                     return false;
00702 
00703                 // now we have the same day
00704                 break;
00705         }
00706 
00707         // the millis decides:
00708         return (calMillis < millis);
00709     }
00710 
00716     public synchronized int hashCode()
00717     {
00718         return rawOffset
00719             ^ (useDaylight
00720                 ? startMonth ^ startDay ^ startDayOfWeek ^ startTime ^ endMonth
00721                 ^ endDay ^ endDayOfWeek ^ endTime : 0);
00722     }
00723 
00728     public String toString()
00729     {
00730         // the test for useDaylight is an incompatibility to jdk1.2, but
00731         // I think this shouldn't hurt.
00732         return getClass().getName() + "[" + "id=" + getID() + ",offset="
00733             + rawOffset + ",dstSavings=" + dstSavings + ",useDaylight="
00734             + useDaylight
00735             + (useDaylight
00736                 ? ",startYear=" + startYear + ",startMode=" + startMode
00737                 + ",startMonth=" + startMonth + ",startDay=" + startDay
00738                 + ",startDayOfWeek=" + startDayOfWeek + ",startTime="
00739                 + startTime + ",startTimeMode=" + startTimeMode + ",endMode="
00740                 + endMode + ",endMonth=" + endMonth + ",endDay=" + endDay
00741                 + ",endDayOfWeek=" + endDayOfWeek + ",endTime=" + endTime
00742                 + ",endTimeMode=" + endTimeMode : "") + "]";
00743     }
00744 }
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:25 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
